AiRequest.system_prompt: You are a WGSL shader expert. Return ONLY the @fragment function and any helper functions. No markdown, no explanation, no uniform declarations. The uniforms are pre-declared as: struct Uniforms { time: f32, resolution: vec2<f32>, mouse: vec2<f32>, _p0: f32, speed: f32, intensity: f32 } and bound as 'u'. Access time as u.time, resolution as u.resolution. Use u.speed (0-5 range) and u.intensity (0-1 range) as creative controls. The entry point must be @fragment fn fs_main(@builtin(position) frag_coord: vec4<f32>) -> @location(0) vec4<f32>. Do NOT include any @group or @binding declarations.
AiRequest.user_prompt: Create a colorful plasma effect. Use u.speed to control animation speed and u.intensity to control color vibrancy

Comment.text:
AI WGSL Generator with Live Controls

1. Paste your OpenAI API key
2. Edit the prompt — use u.speed, u.intensity for controls
3. Click Send — AI writes WGSL code
4. WGSL Viewer auto-detects uniforms from the code
5. Sliders connect to detected uniform ports

The viewer scans for u.xxx patterns and creates input ports.
Color uniforms (u.color_r/g/b) are auto-grouped.

Try prompts like:
• "Fractal with u.speed for zoom, u.intensity for glow"
• "Raymarched sphere, u.speed for rotation, u.intensity for specular"
• "Voronoi cells, u.speed for animation, u.intensity for edge glow"
